title: campylospermum auriculatum
intro: Campylospermum auriculatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the ear-shaped camphor tree due to its distinctive ear-shaped leaves. The plant is traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and bark of the plant are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and are also valued for their potential health benefits such as antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. The herbal preparations of Campylospermum auriculatum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of the treated conditions.
